body: `render_layer` renders only a vertically clipped slice (`oh = visible_h`) but then passes `src_y_start` as `scroll_y` into `render_commands`. Because `render_commands` maps y via `y.saturating_sub(scroll_y)` and `draw_rect`/`draw_scaled_image` take `u32` coordinates (no negative), any command that starts above `src_y_start` but overlaps the visible slice (e.g. backgrounds/borders) will be shifted to y=0 and drawn with the wrong portion of its content. Consider switching to signed y/explicit clipping in `render_commands` (and/or `FontContext::draw_text`/image drawing), or render into an offscreen buffer whose coordinate space starts at y=0 of the layer and blend only the visible rows.

diff_hunk:
@@ -136,6 +137,23 @@ struct ElementIdentity {
     attributes: BTreeMap<String, String>,
 }
 
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
+struct AncestorSlot {
+    element: ElementIdentity,
+    sibling_index: usize,
+    sibling_count: usize,
+    /// Shared full sibling list for this parent's children.
+    siblings: Rc<[ElementIdentity]>,
+    /// How many entries in `siblings` precede this slot's element.
body:
`AncestorSlot.siblings` is documented as a “shared full sibling list”, but the code stores only `Rc::from(preceding_siblings)` (a per-element clone of the preceding-sibling slice). This makes the doc misleading and also defeats the intended sharing/efficiency (each element allocates and duplicates data). Either update the doc/type to reflect that this is only the preceding-sibling list, or thread the parent’s full `Rc<[ElementIdentity]>` down and use `prec_count`/`sibling_index` to slice without per-element cloning.

This issue also appears on line 693 of the same file.

`Rc::from([])` in the synthetic `AncestorSlot` allocations happens for every `Selector::matches` call. This likely allocates a new empty `Rc` each time and can add overhead in selector matching hot paths. Consider avoiding the allocation (e.g. make `siblings` optional, or reuse a shared empty `Rc<[ElementIdentity]>`).
